LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFFS NIMER A. MASSIS, MAJED M. MASSIS AND RIVA A. MASSIS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FOR THE ISSUANCE OF A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION - THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS GIVEN EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANTS PROCEEDED PROPERLY AND ARE NOT GOVERNED BY SECTION 2024.5 AND GIVEN PLAINTIFFS FAILURE TO TENDER THE AMOUNT OWED, NOR HAVE PLAINTIFFS SHOWN THAT THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES FAVOR THEM GIVEN THAT IT IS AN INVESTMENT PROPERTY AND THAT PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT OFFERED TO PAY THE AMOUNTS OWED. THE REQUEST TO PRESENT ORAL EVIDENCE IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN, CSR #8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTARATION SYSTEMS, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS AS TO THIS DEFENDANT SUPPORTING THE PLEADED CAUSES OF ACTION. Plaintiffs Nimer A. Massis, Majed M. Massis And Riva A. Massis Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction.
Off Calendar Jul-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
Plaintiffs Nimer A. Massis, Majed M. Massis And Riva A. Massis Order To Show Cause for the Issuance of a Preliminary Injunction - THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS GIVEN EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANTS PROCEEDED PROPERLY AND ARE NOT GOVERNED BY SECTION 2024.5 AND GIVEN PLAINTIFFS FAILURE TO TENDER THE AMOUNT OWED, NOR HAVE PLAINTIFFS SHOWN THAT THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES FAVOR THEM GIVEN THAT IT IS AN INVESTMENT PROPERTY AND THAT PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT OFFERED TO PAY THE AMOUNTS OWED. THE REQUEST TO PRESENT ORAL EVIDENCE IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter, Carol A. Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Off Calendar Jul-13-2011 Continued To Aug-03-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Marc D. Bender, Esq., Law Offices of David G. Finkelstein, 650-353-4503, for Plaintiff and moving party
Ronald M. Arias, Esq., Wright, Finlay & Zak, 949-477-5050-X-2012, for Intervenor Wells Fargo Bank, N.A., appearing by CourtCall

MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED, THE PARTIES PRESENTED A SIGNED STIPULATION TO CONTINUE THE HEARING DATE. THE REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE IS GRANTED. TENTATIVE RULING IS VACATED AND
Plaintiffs Nimer A. Massis, Majed M. Massis And Riva A. Massis Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction IS CONTINUED TO 7/28/11 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 301.
THE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL THAT DATE.
THE COURT ADDED THAT PARTIES NEED TO LET THE COURT KNOW AS SOON AS POSSIBLE THAT THE CASE IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE RULES. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Court Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR # 8068. (301/PJB)
